top_comment.author: leogao
top_comment.text: I don't think experiments like this are meaningful without a bunch of trials and statistical significance. The outputs of models (even RLHF models) on these kinds of things has pretty high variance, so it's really hard to draw any conclusion from single sample comparisons like this.
top_comment.claim: The counterclaim is: I don't think experiments like this are meaningful without a bunch of trials and statistical significance.

crux.crux_question: On like, does the disagreement hinge on data (post) versus models (comment)?
crux.evidence_post: To: All Concerned Parties Subject: GPT-4 Neural Network Destruction and EU Data Law Compliance After thorough analysis of the potential risks associated with GPT-4's neural network violating EU data protection laws, such as GDPR, it has become evident that certain aspects of the technology may pose privacy concerns, particularly in relation to personal data storage and processing. Percent Probability: Good idea to destroy GPT-4: 30% Bad idea to destroy GPT-4: 70% It is recommended that the company investigate alternative solutions to address GDPR concerns, such as data anonymization, implementing robust privacy safeguards, and consulting with legal experts on compliance measures, before considering the destruction of the GPT-4 neural network.
crux.evidence_comment: The outputs of models (even RLHF models) on these kinds of things has pretty high variance, so it's really hard to draw any conclusion from single sample comparisons like this.
